text.title: Cloud LB Terraform Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for provisioning cloud LBs via Terraform safely. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every production `aws_lb` resource MUST have `enable_deletion_protection = true`. GCP `google_compute_global_forwarding_rule` MUST have `lifecycle { prevent_destroy = true }`.

rationale.source: AWS ELB best practices + Terraform lifecycle docs

rule.rationale: A stray `terraform destroy` (wrong workspace, careless module change) can delete a production LB in seconds. Deletion protection is the only mechanism that survives operator error.

rule.statement: HTTPS listeners MUST use `ssl_policy = "ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06"` (AWS) or the GCP `MODERN` SSL policy. TLS < 1.2 listeners are forbidden.

rationale.source: PCI-DSS v4.0 + Mozilla SSL config generator

rule.rationale: TLS 1.0/1.1 have known weaknesses (BEAST, POODLE) and are non-compliant with PCI-DSS v4 and FedRAMP. Modern profile enforces TLS 1.2+ with AEAD ciphers.

rule.statement: Every LB MUST emit access logs to a dedicated, versioned object store: S3 bucket with bucket policy for `elasticloadbalancing.amazonaws.com` (AWS), or `google_storage_bucket` with `log_config.enable = true` and `sample_rate = 1.0` (GCP).

rationale.source: AWS ELB access logs docs + Cloud Logging docs

rule.rationale: Without access logs you cannot diagnose 5xx storms, attribute traffic spikes, or detect WAF-evading attackers. Versioned bucket lets you replay logs even if attackers tamper with newer objects.

rule.statement: Public-facing HTTP listeners (port 80) MUST be either absent or configured to return a 301 redirect to the HTTPS listener (`type = "redirect"`). Forwarding plain HTTP to backends is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mixing HTTP and HTTPS routes leaks session cookies, allows MITM credential stealing, and breaks HSTS preload eligibility.

rule.statement: ALB security group ingress MUST be limited to ports 80 + 443 from `0.0.0.0/0` (or narrower for internal LBs). Backend security groups MUST allow ingress only from the LB security group, not from `0.0.0.0/0` and not from a CIDR.
rule.rationale: Open backend security groups defeat the LB's purpose. Allowing all ports to the LB enables port-scan amplification.
